bio: Ali Younessi served as Iran's Minister of Intelligence for most of [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami)'s presidency (1999–2005), replacing [Qorbanali Dorri-Najafabadi](/people/qorbanali-dorri-najafabadi) who resigned over the 1998 chain murders of dissidents. A cleric and jurist, Younessi was seen as a reformist appointment and took steps to investigate intelligence operations that had targeted journalists and intellectuals. He was later appointed as [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani)'s special assistant for ethnic minorities and religious affairs (2014–2021). In 2020 he was arrested on charges of espionage but released.
editorial_notes: Born 1956. Intelligence Minister 1999–2005. Special assistant to Rouhani 2014–2021. Arrested on espionage charges 2020, later released.
